reconfigure.title: Reconfigure VistaPool Connection
reconfigure.description: Update the connection settings for your VistaPool controller.

data.modbus_framer: Protocol framing: 'tcp' = Modbus TCP (MBAP header, for gateways with conversion), 'rtu' = RTU over TCP (for transparent TCP gateways)

data_description.host: Enter the IP address of the Modbus TCP gateway connected to your pool controller.
data_description.port: Standard Modbus TCP port. Change only if your gateway uses a non-standard port.
data_description.slave_id: The Modbus slave address of the pool controller. Most setups use 1.
data_description.modbus_framer: Select 'tcp' for gateways that convert between TCP and RTU. Select 'rtu' for transparent gateways that forward raw RTU frames over TCP.

user.title: VistaPool Connection
user.description: Configure the connection to your VistaPool controller.

data.name: Device name (used as entity ID prefix).
data.modbus_framer: Protocol framing: 'tcp' = Modbus TCP (MBAP header, for gateways with conversion), 'rtu' = RTU over TCP (for transparent TCP gateways)
data.use_filtration1: Enable 1st filtration timer for automatic mode
data.use_filtration2: Enable 2nd filtration timer for automatic mode
data.use_filtration3: Enable 3rd filtration timer for automatic mode

data_description.name: Used as a prefix for all entity IDs. Spaces become underscores, e.g. 'Pool West' → sensor.pool_west_measure_ph.
data_description.host: Enter the IP address of the Modbus TCP gateway connected to your pool controller.
data_description.port: Standard Modbus TCP port. Change only if your gateway uses a non-standard port.
data_description.scan_interval: How often the integration reads data from the pool controller.
data_description.slave_id: The Modbus slave address of the pool controller. Most setups use 1.
data_description.modbus_framer: Select 'tcp' for gateways that convert between TCP and RTU. Select 'rtu' for transparent gateways that forward raw RTU frames over TCP.
data_description.use_filtration1: Creates timer entities for configuring automatic filtration schedule.
data_description.use_filtration2: Creates timer entities for configuring automatic filtration schedule.
data_description.use_filtration3: Creates timer entities for configuring automatic filtration schedule.
data_description.use_light: Creates entities to control and monitor the pool light relay.
data_description.use_cover_sensor: Creates a binary sensor indicating whether the pool cover is open or closed.
data_description.filtration_pump_power: Set the rated wattage of the filtration pump. When non-zero, two sensors are created: instantaneous power (W) and cumulative energy (Wh), both usable in the Energy dashboard.

import_from_vistapool.title: Import from existing VistaPool integration
import_from_vistapool.description:
Detected an existing VistaPool integration entry **{entry_title}** (legacy `vistapool` domain).

Click **Submit** to migrate it to the new `neopool` domain in one step:

- All entity IDs, history and customizations are preserved
- Connection settings (host, port, slave ID, options) are copied automatically
- Device area assignment, custom name and labels are restored
- The leftover `custom_components/vistapool/` folder is cleaned up

If you instead want to add a *separate* NeoPool device, cancel and first remove the legacy VistaPool integration manually.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the specified address and port.
error.cannot_read_modbus: Connected, but cannot read from the Modbus device. Check slave ID and framer settings.
error.serial_mismatch: The device at this address has a different serial number than the one originally configured.
error.name_already_used: This device name is already in use. Please choose a different name.

abort.entry_not_found: The integration entry could not be found.
abort.migration_complete: Migration completed successfully. Your VistaPool integration was moved to the new NeoPool domain. Please **restart Home Assistant** to clean up the old integration loaded in memory.
abort.migration_failed:
Migration failed: {error}

Check the Home Assistant logs and report the issue on GitHub. The legacy VistaPool integration is unaffected and continues to work.

init.title: VistaPool Settings
init.description: WARNING: Changing enabled relays will automatically reload the integration! Changes take effect immediately.

data.measure_when_filtration_off: Measure values even when filtration is off
data.use_filtration1: Enable 1st filtration timer for automatic mode
data.use_filtration2: Enable 2nd filtration timer for automatic mode
data.use_filtration3: Enable 3rd filtration timer for automatic mode

data_description.scan_interval: Lower values increase responsiveness but generate more Modbus traffic.
data_description.timer_resolution: Determines the granularity of time selections in timer entities.
data_description.measure_when_filtration_off: When disabled, chemical measurements (pH, ORP, etc.) are only taken while the filtration pump is running.
data_description.use_filtration1: Creates timer entities for configuring automatic filtration schedule.
data_description.use_filtration2: Creates timer entities for configuring automatic filtration schedule.
data_description.use_filtration3: Creates timer entities for configuring automatic filtration schedule.
data_description.use_light: Creates entities to control and monitor the pool light relay.
data_description.use_cover_sensor: Creates a binary sensor and enables cover-related entities (hydrolysis cover reduction, shutdown temperature).
data_description.use_aux1: Creates switch and timer entities for this auxiliary relay output.
data_description.use_aux2: Creates switch and timer entities for this auxiliary relay output.
data_description.use_aux3: Creates switch and timer entities for this auxiliary relay output.
data_description.use_aux4: Creates switch and timer entities for this auxiliary relay output.
data_description.unlock_advanced: Enter the unlock code to access advanced settings (format: device_prefix + current year).
data_description.enable_backwash_option: Adds 'Backwash' to the filtration mode select. Only for systems with manual multi-way valves.
data_description.filtration_pump_power: Set the rated wattage of the filtration pump. When non-zero, two sensors are created: instantaneous power (W) and cumulative energy (Wh), both usable in the Energy dashboard.

advanced.title: Advanced Settings
advanced.description: ⚠️ WARNING: Incorrect use may damage filtration!

data_description.enable_backwash_option: Adds 'Backwash' to the filtration mode select. Use with caution — improper use may damage your filtration system.
data_description.dev_overrides_enabled: When enabled, values from the override JSON are injected into coordinator data for testing.
data_description.dev_overrides: A JSON object where each key is a Modbus register name and each value overrides the actual reading.

error.unlock_advanced_error: Invalid password. Try again or remove the password.

state.ph_over: pH higher than the set point
state.ph_under: pH lower than the set point

hidro_temp_shutdown.name: Enable hydrolysis shutdown on high temperature

set_timer.name: Set timer
set_timer.description: Set or update a timer on the pool controller.

entry_id.name: Entry ID
entry_id.description: The unique ID of the integration entry. Optional.

timer.name: Timer name
timer.description: The timer identifier (e.g., filtration1, filtration2, etc.).

write_register.name: Write register
write_register.description: Write a value to a Modbus holding register using FC16 with read-back verification.

entry_id.name: Entry ID
entry_id.description: The unique ID of the integration entry. Optional.

address.name: Register address
address.description: Modbus register address (decimal or hex, e.g. 1539 or 0x0603).

value.name: Value
value.description: Value to write (0-65535, decimal or hex).

apply.name: Apply
apply.description: Save to EEPROM and execute after write (default: true).

entry_not_found.message: No VistaPool config entry found for entry_id {entry_id}

no_loaded_entry.message: No loaded VistaPool config entry available

no_coordinator.message: No VistaPool coordinator found for entry_id {entry_id}

invalid_apply.message: Invalid apply {apply}: must be a boolean (true/false)

write_verification_failed.message: Write verification failed at {address}: wrote {value}, read back {confirmed}

invalid_register_type.message: Invalid {name} {value}: use a decimal number or hex (0x…)

invalid_register_float.message: Invalid {name} {value}: use an integer, not a float

corrupted_gpio.title: Corrupted GPIO register(s) detected
corrupted_gpio.description:
The following GPIO register(s) on your pool controller contain invalid values:

{details}

This typically happens when the Modbus gateway framing mode does not match the integration's framer setting. The affected function(s) will not work correctly until the register(s) are restored to valid values.

See the integration documentation for repair instructions.

domain_renamed.title: Integration domain migrated to neopool
domain_renamed.description:
The integration domain has been renamed from `vistapool` to `neopool` to avoid a clash with the new core `vistapool` integration introduced in Home Assistant 2026.6.

**{entries} config entr(ies)** and **{entities} entit(ies)** were migrated automatically. Your `entity_id`s, history, automations and dashboards continue to work without changes.

**Old folder cleanup:** `{folder_cleaned}` (if `no`, please remove `custom_components/vistapool/` manually).

**Please restart Home Assistant** to complete the migration.

domain_rename_partial_failure.title: Migration to neopool partially failed
domain_rename_partial_failure.description:
Some VistaPool config entries could not be migrated to the new `neopool` domain:

```
{errors}
```

If your pool controller is offline, deferred entries will retry on the next Home Assistant restart. Other failures should be investigated in the logs and reported on GitHub.
